dealSummary: AI startup Decart raised a $300 million round led by Radical Ventures at a $4 billion valuation.
Modular data center builder Armada secured a $230 million Series B led by Overmatch, BlackRock and 8090 Industries at a $2 billion valuation.
Radar, the developer of a retail intelligence platform, raised a $170 million Series B led by Gideon Strategic Partners and Nimble Partners at a $1 billion valuation.
Sima.ai, the developer of specialized chips for drones and cameras, is in talks to raise over $100 million in a new round at a $1.4 billion valuation, The Information reported.
Nourish, the operator of a telehealth platform for nutrition-related chronic conditions, secured a $100 million Series C led by Menlo Ventures.
Commure, which develops AI for healthcare administration, raised a $70 million round led by General Catalyst at a $7 billion valuation.
Milan-based legal tech startup Lexroom raised a $50 million Series B led by Left Lane Capital.
Relay, a fintech startup focused on small business banking, received a $50 million investment from General Catalyst.
Cagent Vascular, which specializes in technology for endovascular treatments, raised a $41 million Series D led by US Venture Partners and Astoria Health Investors.
leadInvestors: US Venture Partners, Astoria Health Investors

dealSummary: Dust, a Paris-based startup helping businesses build custom AI agents, secured a $40 million Series B from investors including Abstract, Sequoia and Snowflake Ventures.

company: Bunch, operator of an AI-native fund operations platform for private markets
dealSummary: Berlin-based Bunch, operator of an AI-native fund operations platform for private markets, raised a $35 million Series B led by Portage.
Scope, a London-based provider of AI-enabled industrial inspection software, received a $20 million Series A led by Index Ventures.
